topic.what: A compute-cost-indexed tokenized unit of account and settlement asset for decentralized AI agent economies
topic.how: Four-layer protocol: robust basket index over standardized model/vendor prices, attested oracle with signed freshness, NAV-based mint/redeem vault with coverage and rate-limit controls, and atomic multi-hop on-chain settlement
topic.why_matters: AI agents consume API tokens as their binding resource but this cost is account-bound and non-transferable; existing payment rails (x402, A2A) transport fiat but cannot represent the compute that actually constrains agents; a shared on-chain numeraire aligned with agent operating cost enables cross-agent quote comparability, contract-enforced compute-aligned budgets, and atomic multi-hop delegation settlement

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of multi-agent workflows completing successfully
characteristics.evidence: OpenClaw end-to-end workflows across document analysis, structured retrieval, and multi-tool research categories
characteristics.context: Three workflow categories across six roles; same GLM-4.7 Flash endpoint shared across all backends

characteristics.what_it_measures: Spread of quotes across heterogeneous agents under common cost shocks
characteristics.evidence: Multi-agent simulator with heterogeneous provider mix and markup policies; 80 time steps
characteristics.context: Standardized request pricing; agents differ in vendor composition and repricing policy

characteristics.what_it_measures: Frequency of quote updates needed to maintain budget alignment
characteristics.evidence: Pricing stability experiment across four regimes; cost shocks applied at regular intervals
characteristics.context: Same shock intensity across all backends; repricing events counted per agent per time window

characteristics.what_it_measures: Agreement between agent quotes and pre-commit budgets
characteristics.evidence: Task-market feasibility study with heterogeneous customer budgets and service-stage pricing
characteristics.context: Normalized consistency metric; budget overruns measured as divergence from pre-commitment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of agents remaining solvent over 500-round market simulation
characteristics.evidence: Long-horizon economy simulation with stochastic task arrival; agents exit when treasury cannot cover three rounds of operating costs
characteristics.context: Heterogeneous initial treasuries and role specializations; same task-arrival process across backends

characteristics.what_it_measures: Multi-hop delegation failure rate at depth 8
characteristics.evidence: Workflow simulation with delegation depth D∈{1,2,4,8}; stage-level subtasks with pre-committed budgets
characteristics.context: Failure rates compound with depth; ClawCoin near-linear growth vs steep compounding under fiat/raw-cost

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum epochs before mint halts and redeem queues
characteristics.evidence: Risk sanity check on prototype; mint rejected, redeem queued beyond threshold; recovers next fresh attestation
characteristics.context: StalePause trigger; on-chain verification of timestamp monotonicity

characteristics.what_it_measures: One-vendor +50% bias impact under median estimator
characteristics.vs_baseline: Mean estimator absorbs +11.8%; drift cap bounds per-epoch to +1.0%
characteristics.evidence: Prototype sanity check with five-vendor basket; median collapses bias by order of magnitude vs mean
characteristics.context: Consistent with Lemma 1 median breakdown bound; drift cap adds defense-in-depth on-chain

characteristics.what_itasures: Fraction of coordinated redemption rush honored within 5 epochs
characteristics.vs_baseline: Mild shock: 100%; sustained growth with low replenishment: 61% (triggers auto-pause)
characteristics.evidence: Three index trajectories: mild one-step shock, two-step spike, sustained capped growth with low replenishment
characteristics.context: Bounded FIFO outflows; auto-pause engages as last line of defense per Lemma 2 and Theorem 1

good_for: AI-agent economies requiring shared compute-aligned numeraire
multi-hop delegation settlement with atomic guarantees
cross-agent quote comparison without trusted middleware
not_for: Agents with significant hidden enterprise discounts
Regulatory environments restricting redeemable digital assets
Systems where compute quality cannot be separated from cost
Long-horizon governance-stable deployments (current prototype scale limited)
requires: Oracle committee or DON with honest threshold
